rows.notes: Page title / section heading at top. Handwriting is legible but the Latin/archaic spelling is slightly unclear (e.g. 'Arrearagia') so given less than perfect confidence.

rows.description: Sayer (olim Myne) Capons incl: pro Anno -
rows.notes: Numbers well aligned in the pounds/shillings/pence columns. Pounds appears as '01' read as 1; shillings 8 and pence 4 are clear and within valid ranges.

rows.description: Hertford College (olim Hart Hall) pro Bien:
rows.notes: Description and amounts are clear. Pound = '03' (read as 3), shillings 6 and pence 8 are crisply written and valid.
Amounts aligned and clear: 09 0 0 (read as 9 pounds, 0s, 0d). Description legible enough to identify name and 'pro an'.

Amounts (13 / 6 / 8) are clear and validate correctly. The word between 'pro' and 'Biennio' is heavily scratched out; description transcribed indicating the scribble. Slight uncertainty only in the crossed text, not the numbers.
Clear, centered small header introducing the following group of entries ('Little Pew'). No amounts present.

Pounds (11) and shillings (19) are clear. Pence reads as '10' with a small superscript/mark interpreted as a three‑quarters symbol (¾ => 0.75). The fractional symbol is faint/ambiguous, so confidence is moderate.

Pounds 0 (written as '0'), shillings 15 and pence '10' are readable. As with the previous row there is a small fractional mark after the pence that appears to be ¾ (three farthings) but is faint — interpreted as 0.75 with moderate confidence.

A subtotal line (no left description) under the Little Pew entries. Pounds (308) and shillings (13) are legible; pence shows '6' with a small fractional mark similar to ¾ seen above — interpreted as 0.75. The fraction on this larger total is somewhat faint, so moderate confidence.
Clear handwriting and numbers: 2 pounds, 12 shillings, 2 pence. All values fall within valid ranges and align with the ruling lines.

Description is legible though the first word might be 'Thorp' or 'Thrup' — amount 12 pounds, 10 shillings, 0 pence is clearly written and validates correctly. Slight uncertainty only in the exact spelling of the first word.
Final total under the two small entries (Widow Ryman + Thrup Annuity). The sum (15 2 2) is clearly written, aligned and internally consistent with the two rows above (2 12 2 + 12 10 0 = 15 2 2).
